canonical_definition: A family of distributional intervention procedures introduced in the paper to measure global and local statistical affinity between words and contexts in RoBERTa.
papers: Language Models as Causal Effect Generators
SAE-SSV: Supervised Steering in Sparse Representation Spaces for Reliable Control of Language Models

definitions.text: A family of distributional intervention procedures introduced in the paper to measure global and local statistical affinity between words and contexts in RoBERTa.
definitions.paper: Language Models as Causal Effect Generators

definitions.text: A causal probing procedure used to assess whether masked language models encode constructional constraints through changes in output distributions under masking.
definitions.paper: SAE-SSV: Supervised Steering in Sparse Representation Spaces for Reliable Control of Language Models

definitions.text: A bidirectional-context evaluation procedure that quantifies how strongly a model assigns probability to words or word pairs in masked strings.
